intro: Grastidium papyraceum, also known as the paper grass or paperbark, is a small, annual herb native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as respiratory problems, colds, and fever due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Grastidium papyraceum include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and cough drops, which are often used to soothe sore throats and coughs.
